construct.label: Economic and vocational anxiety
construct.definition: Anxiety arising from the belief that advancing AI systems may reduce the future value, bargaining power, status, or transferability of occupational skills.
construct.subconstruct: wage and bargaining-power pressure in customer support

scenario_text: Imagine that a large customer-support outsourcing firm deploys an AI assistant that resolves most routine chat and email cases without human agents. Human support workers remain responsible for difficult complaints, escalations, and sensitive cases, but supervisors state that each agent can now handle far more accounts. New contracts are bid at lower prices, hiring slows, and workers report that requests for raises are increasingly met with comparisons to automated support costs.

response_scale.prompt: How anxious would this scenario make you about AI support automation reducing customer-support workers' wages or bargaining power?

scoring.missing_response_rule: Treat skipped responses as missing at the item level. Do not impute single-item scores for primary analyses.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude respondents who fail the survey-level attention check specified in the study protocol.
Exclude respondents who complete the item in less than one-third of the median reading time for the administration wave.
Exclude respondents who report that they could not understand the scenario after reading it.
Exclude respondents younger than the minimum eligible age for the study population.

interpretation_bands.label: low
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports little or no anxiety about AI support automation weakening wages or bargaining power.

interpretation_bands.label: moderate
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports a meaningful but not severe anxiety response to customer-support wage pressure from AI automation.

interpretation_bands.label: high
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports strong anxiety that AI support automation could weaken customer-support compensation or bargaining power.

population.target_population: Adults in the general population, with planned subgroup analysis for customer-support workers, call-center workers, and respondents in service occupations.
population.eligible_respondents: Respondents aged 18 or older who can complete the survey language without assistance and provide informed consent.
exclusion_criteria: Current inability to provide informed consent.
Duplicate panel participation within the same measurement wave.
Self-reported inability to read the survey language.

administration_mode.instructions: Present the scenario text exactly as written, followed immediately by the response prompt and the five ordered response options.
administration_mode.randomization: Randomize item order within the economic_vocational domain block while preserving the ordered response scale.
administration_mode.longitudinal_use: Use identical wording, response anchors, scoring, and eligibility rules across waves unless a new item version is released.

ethics_flags.data_protection_notes: Do not collect employer names, customer records, union membership details, wage records, or identifiable workplace conflict information for this item.
ethics_flags.irb_notes: The item presents a plausible compensation-pressure scenario and may induce mild concern about wages or bargaining power. Include a debrief clarifying that the scenario is hypothetical and not a prediction about any respondent's employer.
